# Obligatory Java to Python Migration Good Practices

## Core standards

- Preserve behavior first with tests before refactoring
- Convert large Java classes into focused Python modules
- Use Pythonic properties only when needed
- Use type hints and dataclasses for explicit models
- Convert checked-exception flows to specific Python exceptions
- Prefer Python idioms and standard libraries over Java patterns
- Follow Python naming conventions (`snake_case`, `PascalCase`)
- Re-validate performance-sensitive paths post-migration
- Run linting, tests, and static checks for each migration batch

## Minimal done criteria

1. Migration batch compiles/lints cleanly
2. Relevant tests pass and behavior is preserved
3. Naming and exception handling follow Python conventions
